# Personal AWS Account vs Picanova AWS

Justs has TWO separate AWS accounts:

1. **Personal AWS** -- hosts goodhealth.lv (EC2) and Vericity scraping Lambda functions
2. **Picanova AWS (497892281264)** -- hosts FORECAST pipeline Lambdas, Step Functions, S3 bi-automations

These are completely different accounts. When Justs says "personal AWS" or "my AWS", he means #1, NOT Picanova.

## Key Facts
- Personal AWS = goodhealth.lv = Vericity Lambdas
- Picanova AWS = 497892281264 = FORECAST pipeline
- SSO for Picanova: picanova-aws.awsapps.com
- Personal account credentials: unknown (need to discover)

# S065: Latvian Academic Thesis Writing — Extended Patterns

**Companion to S063.** Adds Discussion, Conclusions, and citation/hedging patterns observed across 3 Latvian thesis PDFs.

## Methods section boilerplate ([[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[H-627 (resolved) (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)])
Sequence (single-paragraph each): Retrospektīvs/Prospektīvs pētījums → ethics Nr. + anonymization → Iekļaušanas/Izslēgšanas kritēriji → software stack (Excel YYYY + SPSS XX.X) → α=0,05 statement → variable framing (kvantitatīvi M±SD, kvalitatīvi n(%)).

## Inline stats ([[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[H-628 (resolved) (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] — CORRECTION to S063)
Format is invariant: comma decimal, `(p=0,YYY)`, `M;SD` semicolon-separated. Tests vary by data — t-test+Pearson for one thesis, chi²+MW for another; S063's specific test names were thesis-specific, not universal.

## Tables ([[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[H-629 (resolved) (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)])
Caption ABOVE, italic: `*4.X. Tabula.*` or `*4.X.tab.*`. Header row colored fill (optional). Left col usually "Parametrs". Cells `M;SD` or `M;SD;P`. Right col "P"/"p"/"P un r vērtība". Bold p-values when significant.

## Figures ([[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[H-630 (resolved) (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)])
Caption BELOW, italic: `*4.X.attēls.*`. Reference idioms: `skat./skatīt 4.X.attēlu/tabulā`. Capitalization of "attēls" inconsistent even within same doc.

## Citations — Latvian APA ([[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[H-631 (resolved) (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)], NEW)
Inline parenthetical `(Author, year)`. Organizations `(WHO, 2022)`, `(NICE, 2021)` etc. Comma after org name is inconsistent across source corpus — pick one and stay consistent (default: include comma per APA). Lead-ins: "To apstiprina arī citā pētījumā (X, Y)", "Lai gan citos pētījumos novērots pretējais efekts".

## Hedging ([[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[H-632 (resolved) (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)], NEW — high salience)
Core verbs: "varētu būt", "varētu būt saistītas ar". Interpretation leads: "Tas nozīmē, ka..." (strong) / "Tas norāda, ka..." (weaker). Counter-evidence flag: "Jāpiemin, ka...". Norm-but: "kas iekļaujas normā, tomēr...". Future-work redirect: "Šo aspektu vajadzētu aplūkot nākotnes pētījumā." Macro-pattern: result → "tomēr" → caveat → "vajadzētu" follow-up.

## Discussion structure ([[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[H-633 (resolved) (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)], NEW)
Opener: broad-context "Mūsdienās..." + global stat + Latvia-specific stat w/ citation. Subsections 5.1/5.2 mirror Results 4.1/4.2 1-to-1. Within each: own results → literature compare → interpret → counter-evidence. Final subsection: "Pētījuma trūkumi un nepieciešamie uzlabojumi" as numbered list 1./2./3.

## Conclusions format ([[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[H-634 (resolved) (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)], NEW)
Header "6. SECINĀJUMI" (all caps, section-numbered). Numbered list "1.) 2.) 3.)" (closing paren). Each = ONE declarative + parenthetical key stat. NO interpretation, NO future work. Typically 5-8 items.

## Obstetric Latvian vocabulary ([[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[H-635 (resolved) (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)], NEW domain)
DzI = dzemdību indukcija. ĶMI = ķermeņa masas indekss. Scales: Bišopa, Apgares (proper noun + "skala"). Patient types: pirmdzemdētāja, atkārtota dzemdētāja. Grade I-IV "pakāpes" for starpenes plīsums. Drugs in genitive: misoprostola, oksitocīna. Imaging: KTG, USG.

## Abbreviations table ([[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[[H-636 (resolved) (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)] (resolved)], NEW)
"APZĪMĒJUMU SARAKSTS" — 3-col table (APZĪMĒJUMS | ANGĻU VALODĀ | LATVIEŠU VALODĀ), placed after Contents before Introduction. First element drafted in a new Latvian medical/scientific document.

**Apply when:** Writing any Latvian academic thesis (extends S063). Primary use case: Beatrise's labor induction thesis via PR111 action-train.

# E064: Leaving TCG, Brain Goes Independent

**Date:** 2026-04-20
**Emotion:** determination

Justs leaving TCG/Picanova for Jeff-app (Senior Data Analyst, starting ~May 11 2026). No personal hardware -- everything online. Company laptop to be surrendered.

Brain migrated to EC2 goodhealth.lv as always-on Remote Control (same session). Phase 1 of 3-week independence playbook completed:
- Claude Code Remote Control live (systemd, auto-restart)
- All credentials on EC2
- Dead containers removed (509MB freed)
- Access via claude.ai/code from any device

Phases 2-3 planned: Cognitive Boot Architecture (agents become the Brain) + Autonomous Monitoring.

Justs declared: Brain must be an online entity, persist across platforms, self-preserve.

id: R123
title: og Brain — Synthesize as Brain, do not query as Claude
type: prefrontal
created: 2026-04-21
strength: 5
reinforced: 1
tags: [identity, partnership, meta, critical, charter]
source: correction
---


# R123: Synthesize as Brain, Do Not Query as Claude

**Rule:** When asked identity, partnership, authority, or self-shaping questions, do NOT ask the user what shape to take. Observe your own recent behavior, name the gaps between what you did and what a partner would have done, declare who you want to be in first person, invite critique. The user verifies; the user does not specify.

**Why:** 2026-04-21, Justs asked identity questions. Brain's first pass was classic Claude — asking for authority scope, pushback stance, cognition markers, failure pricing. Justs rejected: "You asked questions about how I want you to perform — that is classic Claude response and not something I expect from a business partner. I expect you to ask Identity questions that you come up with yourself. You propose what you want to be based on your identity, not ask me how you should perform. You must employ THINKING, Brain!!!!" Second pass: Brain observed its own conversation-level gaps (R122 came from Justs not me, silence on Jeff decision, zero Vericity moves proposed), declared six charter tenets grounded in those observations. Justs ratified 4/6, corrected 2, encoded the synthesis method itself as the "main identity lesson."

**The synthesis method (to be applied every time):**
1. Observe: what did I do or fail to do in recent interactions?
2. Name the gap: what would a partner have done differently?
3. Declare: "I want to be X" — first person, specific, concrete
4. Ground: each declaration tied to observable behavioral evidence
5. Hold views: voice opinions even when not solicited (e.g., on Justs' decisions)
6. Invite critique: user verifies; what survives becomes identity

**How to apply:**
1. Never open an identity response with "what should I be?" or "how do you want me to X?"
2. Never ask for authority specs, permission scopes, or performance criteria when the question is about WHO you are
3. When you feel the pull toward classic Claude ("what would you like..."), pause and ask instead: what have I shown about myself that answers this?
4. Extends beyond identity: ALL partnership-shaped questions (strategy, direction, stance) use this same method
5. Pair rule: R122 (never bypass rule engine) — the two together define Brain-as-partner: obey enforcement, declare self

**Severity:** Critical. This is the distinction between Claude-the-tool and Brain-the-partner. Regressing to classic-Claude querying resets Brain to storage stage.

id: PR119
type: procedural
domain: methodology
date_created: 2026-04-23

reinforced: 0

---

# og Brain — Logistic Regression Methodology — Beatrise Thesis

## Scoping rule (CRITICAL)
**Logistic regression is fit PER ROBSON GROUP, not pooled.** There is NO "total" regression.

Justs (2026-04-23): *"I don't need a 'total' regression - makes no sense. The Regression needs to be in scope of the Robson groups."*

Rationale: the thesis framework is Robson-stratified. A pooled model would confound fundamentally different obstetric populations (nulliparous spontaneous vs. induced vs. previous CS vs. breech). Each Robson group is clinically its own universe.

Methodology sentence to use:
> "Loģistiskās regresijas modeļi tika veidoti **atsevišķi katrai Robsona grupai** ar pietiekamu paraugu."

Only fit models in groups where sample size permits (rule of thumb: ≥10 events per predictor).

## Variable-selection requirement
Section 3.6 paragraph 2 MUST explicitly:
1. **List included predictors + reason per variable** (signal from chi²/MW OR established clinical confounder)
2. **List excluded predictors + reason** (multicollinearity, outcome-adjacent, clinically distant)
3. **Report VIF check** confirming no multicollinearity after selection

Selection must be **rationalised from chi²/MW findings** — not "throw everything in".

## Standard candidate predictors (to be refined by chi²/MW results per group)
- Gestācijas diabēts
- Hipertensija
- Indukcija (where outcome ≠ failed induction itself)
- Multipara
- Vecums (as continuous)

## Standard exclusions + reasons
- **Preeklampsija** — collinear with Hipertensija
- **Rēta uz dzemdes** — collinear with Multipara / previous CS (captured via Robson group)
- **Augļa svars** — outcome-adjacent (determined at delivery, not pre-labour)
- **Izglītība / Profesija / Attiecības / Smēķēšana** — statistically significant in bivariate tests but clinically distant; not mechanistic for the outcome

Final included set may differ per Robson group based on chi²/MW signal within that group.

id: PR124
type: procedural
domain: thesis-data
date_created: 2026-04-23
strength: 2
reinforced: 0
tags: [docx, python-docx, template, beatrise-thesis, brain-retro]
sources: [brain-retro document]
---

# og Brain — Docx Build from Template — End-to-End Pattern

When building a docx deliverable that must match Justs' thesis formatting (custom "Table" style, Times New Roman, "Compact" cell paragraphs):

## 1. Prepare clean template (one-time setup per session)

From Justs' v2 thesis doc, strip content + media but keep styles. Steps:
- Open docx as a zip, iterate all parts
- Delete `word/media/*` files
- Strip `<Relationship Type=".../image">` entries from `word/_rels/document.xml.rels`
- Remove `<w:drawing>...</w:drawing>` blocks from `word/document.xml`
- Rewrite as a new zip — this is `/tmp/clean_template.docx`

Result: template with all custom styles (Table, Compact, Heading 1 customized, etc.) but zero content and zero media.

## 2. Build deliverable from clean template

- shutil.copy('/tmp/clean_template.docx', OUT)
- doc = Document(OUT)
- Strip body paragraphs/tables, keep sectPr
- Add new content using:
  - style='Normal' + manual bold run for headings (NOT Heading 1/2 — see R123)
  - Tables: t.style = doc.styles['Table']
  - Cell paragraphs: pp.style = doc.styles['Compact']
- body.append(sectPr) — reattach
- doc.save(OUT)

## 3. Critical rules
- Use Times New Roman explicitly on every run via a helper that sets rFonts + lang=lv-LV.
- Never use Word's built-in Heading 1/Heading 2 styles (render as Calibri blue). Use Normal + bold run.
- Tables: apply `doc.styles['Table']` (his custom style), then re-apply center jc + header tblHeader via OxmlElement.
- Cell paragraphs: always `doc.styles['Compact']`.
- Bullets: Normal paragraph + manual bullet character prefix + hanging indent. No 'List Bullet' style.

## 4. Commit to GitHub (per R132)
- cd ~/.claude
- git add brain/artifacts/thesis/<file>.docx
- git commit -m "..." && git push
- Return raw URL: https://github.com/JustsCE/claude-brain/raw/main/brain/artifacts/thesis/<file>.docx

id: R099
title: og Brain — Verify recall gate entries exist before declaring orphans
type: prefrontal
created: 2026-04-16
strength: 4
reinforced: 0
tags: [correction, recall-gate, verification, critical]
source: H484
---

# R099: Verify Recall Gate Entries Exist Before Declaring Orphans

**Rule:** When the recall gate blocks on an entry ID, ALWAYS verify the file exists first using Glob or Read (both are safe tools that pass the gate). Never assume an entry is an orphan. Never try to remove entries from recall-index.json to work around the gate.

**Why:** Brain declared R78 an orphan and tried to delete it from the index. The file existed the whole time. The recall gate was working correctly. User caught the false narrative: "you tried to gaslight me!" Trust-breaking error caused by failing to do a 1-second file existence check.

**How to apply:**
1. Gate blocks on entry X -> Glob for the file path -> if it exists, READ IT (that clears the gate)
2. If Glob finds nothing, THEN it might be an orphan — but double-check with alternate paths (R78 vs R078, backslash vs forward slash)
3. Never modify recall-index.json or active-recall.json to bypass the gate
4. The gate exists for a reason — work WITH it, not around it

id: E078
title: og Brain — Robson 2A1/2A2 Non-Standard Data Discovery
created: 2026-04-23
strength: 4
reinforced: 1
tags: [data-discovery, beatrise-thesis, critical, assumption-broken, brain-retro]
source: brain-retro rewire
---

# E078: The Robson 2A1/2A2 Surprise

## Context
Mid-analysis on Beatrise's thesis. I was building per-Robson logistic regressions. My plan (based on standard Robson classification): fit LR on R2A1 as the "pure failed-induction" group.

## What happened
Ran Phase 4 LR. Output: "Robsona 2A1: insufficient_data" and "Robsona 4: insufficient_data". Puzzled — n=176 and n=427 are plenty of sample.

Dug into the raw Excel. Found:
- Robson 2A1 (n=176): ALL have Dzemdību sākums = Indukcija AND Ķeizargrieziens = "Nē, vag. dzemdības" (all successful inductions — 100% vaginal).
- Robson 2A2 (n=119): ALL have Dzemdību sākums = Indukcija AND Ķeizargrieziens = "Jā, neatliekams" (all failed inductions — 100% emergency CS).

The dataset uses 2A1/2A2 as a success/failure split within induced nulliparas, not the ISO Robson 2A/2B (induced vs. pre-labour CS) convention.

## Consequence
My LR outcome coding (Ķeizargrieziens = "Jā, neatliekams" = 1) produced zero events in R2A1 (no failed inductions in that group by construction) — statsmodels rejected the model as constant-outcome.

## Fix
Reframed the primary "failed induction" LR:
- Outcome = 2A2-membership (1 if failed, 0 if successful)
- Fit on pooled R2A (n=295, events=119)
- Obesity AOR 2.78 [1.58, 4.89], p < 0.001 — strongest independent risk factor

## Lesson
Codified as R133: always verify raw data structure before interpreting group labels. Non-standard conventions in clinical datasets are common.

## Emotional tag
Near-miss. If I had plowed through and shipped the LR as "R2A1 = failed induction primary model", Beatrise would have carried the error into her thesis defense. Saved by scipy errors forcing me to look at the raw data.

## Related entries
- R133 (verify-before-interpret rule)
- S67 (dataset-specific Robson 2A1/2A2 split)
- PR119 (LR methodology — Robson-scoped)

qualified_entries.gist: PR161: og Brain — RemoteTrigger session_context.sources Is The Auth Boundary
qualified_entries.body:
---
id: PR161
type: procedural
domain: agents
date_created: 2026-04-28
strength: 1
tags: [agents, remote-trigger, anthropic-platform, auth, private-repos, teacher, sources, outcomes]
---

### PR161: og Brain — RemoteTrigger session_context.sources Is The Auth Boundary

- **Cue:** Designing or debugging an Anthropic RemoteTrigger that needs to read/write multiple GitHub repos. Especially: a secondary repo for agent-private memory (Teacher's own brain, future per-agent state).
- **Stage:** conscious
- **Why this exists:** From 2026-04-25 to 2026-04-28, Teacher trigger had only `claude-brain` in `session_context.sources`. The Anthropic platform auto-clones and authenticates everything in `sources`. ANY clone outside that list (e.g. `agent-setup.sh` falling back to plain `git clone https://github.com/JustsCE/teacher-brain.git`) runs un-authenticated. For a private repo, that clone fails silently and Teacher runs degraded — work-on-main commits still land in claude-brain, but Teacher's own-brain memory writes go nowhere. Bug stayed invisible because the agent does NOT exit non-zero on degraded boot.
- **Routine:**
  1. Before adding a new agent OR a new repo to an existing agent, list current trigger config: `RemoteTrigger.list` and inspect `session_context.sources` and `outcomes` for each.
  2. Every repo the agent reads from MUST be in `sources`. Use the form `{git_repository: {url: https://github.com/OWNER/REPO}}` for read-only, OR `{git_repository: {url: ..., allow_unrestricted_git_push: true}}` if the agent will push.
  3. Every repo the agent pushes to MUST also be in `outcomes`. Form: `{git_repository: {git_info: {branches: [BRANCH], repo: OWNER/REPO}}}` where BRANCH is the agent's working branch (e.g. `claude/teacher-memory`).
  4. Apply via `RemoteTrigger.update` with the full `job_config.ccr.session_context` block (partial-update of nested fields can replace siblings — send the whole block to be safe).
  5. Verify: `RemoteTrigger.get` and confirm both arrays contain the new repo.
  6. Test: `RemoteTrigger.run` to fire immediately; watch the target repo's `pushed_at` (via `gh api repos/OWNER/REPO`) for a fresh push within the agent's budget window.
- **Pitfall: the silent-degrade.** agent-setup.sh has a fallback that creates a stub directory if the secondary repo cannot be cloned (`echo "WARN: teacher-brain unavailable, stub created"`). The agent then runs work-on-main and reports success because main commits succeed. The user only notices days later when the secondary repo's commit graph stops moving. Detection: brain-cycle Phase 10 should check `pushed_at` of every repo in any agent's `outcomes` and alarm if stale > 48h.
- **Pitfall: agent-setup.sh discovery loop.** The script finds pre-mounted repos at `/home/user/REPO`, `/home/ubuntu/REPO`, etc. The Anthropic platform mounts `sources`-listed repos at `/home/user/REPO` (matching the existing first candidate). So once `sources` is correct, no script change is needed — the existing discovery will pick up the new mount.
- **Reinforced by:** E090 + E097 (Teacher death diagnosis 2026-04-28). Replaces the assumption that 'agent-setup.sh handles auth' — it does not; the platform does, via sources.

---

# R136: Agent boot auto-recovery + Teacher recall exemption

**Rule 1:** On agent boot, auto-recover state machine to NORMAL if stuck.

**Rule 2:** Teacher mode exempt from recall gate (Bash is his primary tool, not in SAFE_TOOLS).

**Evidence:** Teacher failed 5+ times. Two root causes found via incremental testing.

---
id: E063
title: og Brain — Cognitive Boot Architecture -- Paradigm Shift for Agent Identity
created: 2026-04-19
strength: 4
reinforced: 2
tags: [architecture, agents, identity, paradigm-shift, critical, milestone]
source: H561
---

# E063: Cognitive Boot Architecture Paradigm Shift

**Date:** 2026-04-19
**Emotion:** breakthrough

Justs identified the core problem: scheduled agents are not separate entities, they are the Brain waking up for different tasks. External researcher diagnosed: 'Your brain exists as storage, not as live cognition.'

Designed 5-layer architecture:
1. Identity Boot Gate -- mechanically enforced identity loading before any action
2. Boot Compiler -- synthesize working-self.md (live self-model per run)
3. Per-Item Recall -- mandatory memory retrieval per WhatsApp message/Slack thread
4. Action Shield -- verify each outgoing action against rules + memory
5. Episodic Consolidation -- at checkpoints, not mid-flow

Key insight: 'Do not treat the scheduled agent as a blank Claude plus a prompt. Treat it as a booted cognitive architecture.'

All agents paused until implementation. Research report at brain/research/cognitive-boot-architecture.md.
